channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Mahomes and Kelce help Chiefs to a 23-14 win over Texans and another AFC title game trip
item.description: atrick Mahomes threw for 177 yards and a touchdown, most of it going to best buddy Travis Kelce, and the Kansas City Chiefs beat the Houston Texans 23-14 on Saturday to advance to the AFC championship game for the seventh consecutive season.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sepp Straka surges to 4-shot lead over Hoffman, Day, Lower through 3 rounds at The American Express
item.description: LA QUINTA, Calif. (AP) — Sepp Straka shot an 8-under 64 on Saturday while his top competitors failed to match his sizzling pace, staking the Austrian to a four-shot lead over Charley Hoffman, Jason Day and Justin Lower after three rounds at The American Express.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US opens 2025 with 3-1 win over Venezuela in friendly missing regular starters
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — This camp for the U.S. men's national team is primarily about giving young players opportunities. And U.S. coach Mauricio Pochettino liked what he saw on that front Saturday.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: California pals Learner Tien, 19, and Alex Michelsen, 20, are 2 of 4 US men in the Australian Open’s 4th round
item.description: By HOWARD FENDRICH MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Learner Tien and Alex Michelsen were born about 15 months apart and first crossed paths a decade ago when they were playing in Southern California tennis tournaments for kids.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Moore sisters reunited in beach volleyball at San Jose State
item.description: Salinas graduates and sisters Sabrina and Kylee Moore will be playing beach volleyball this spring at San Jose State
item.dc:creator: John Devine, The Mercury News

item.title: Fox exec calls Brady rumors 'a never-ending, merry-go-round' of narratives
item.description: Even as Tom Brady goes through his first postseason and prepares to call his first Super Bowl as a television analyst, rumors about his future continue to be rampant, much to the dismay of Fox Sports.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Notre Dame's Charles Jagusah has chance for 1st start of season after dramatic comeback from injury
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — When Charles Jagusah's hopes of opening the season as Notre Dame's starting left tackle ended when he tore his right pectoralis muscle in training camp, others may have given up hope on the second-year lineman returning at any point of the season. Jagusah never gave up.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES ODUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sofia Goggia earns her 4th downhill win in Cortina while Lindsey Vonn is 20th on the Olympic course
item.description: CORTINA D'AMPEZZO, Italy (AP) — Sofia Goggia solidified her status as the favorite for another downhill gold by winning Saturday for the fourth time on the course for next year’s Milan-Cortina Olympics.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DAMPF, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cain Velasquez shooting sentencing pushed back over Racial Justice Act motion
item.description: The former MMA and UFC star pleaded no contest last year to attempted murder and other crimes in a 2022 car chase and shooting; his attorney claims bias in prosecutors' sentencing recommendation.
item.dc:creator: Robert Salonga, The Mercury News

item.title: Bill Belichick's girlfriend seemingly shuts down rumors coach will leave North Carolina for NFL
item.description: The girlfriend of North Carolina Tar Heels coach Bill Belichick poured cold water on the rumors he was going to jump back into the professional ranks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Defending champ Jannik Sinner gets back to the fourth round at the Australian Open
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Defending champion Jannik Sinner beat 46th-ranked Marcos Giron of the U.S. 6-3, 6-4, 6-2 at the Australian Open on Saturday night, reaching the fourth round at Melbourne Park for the fourth time. Sinner stretched his unbeaten run to 17 matches, dating to last season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prized Japanese pitcher Roki Sasaki says he intends to sign with Los Angeles Dodgers
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prized Japanese pitcher Roki Sasaki says in an Instagram post he intends to sign with the World Series champion Los Angeles Dodgers.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sammy Sosa gets warm reception for his reconciliation with the Chicago Cubs
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Sammy Sosa jogged onto the stage, carrying an American flag. The crowd cheered wildly. It was just like old times — only Sosa was dressed in a suit, and the scene occurred in a downtown hotel ballroom instead of Wrigley Field.
item.dc:creator: JAY COHEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Volleyball player who forfeited to SJSU trans athlete speaks out on ruined season after taking 'unfair' losses
item.description: Women's volleyball player Macey Boggs testified at a state senate hearing about the experience of her team forfeiting two games to San Jose State in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Girls Basketball: Rancho Buena Vista 56, La Costa Canyon 51
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)-Girls Basketball on Wednesday evening part of girls and boys doubleheader for league action. Camille Dake, Senior from RBV lead the way for the Longhorns. Great game that ended with the home team winning 56-51.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: San Diego Clippers Hometown Big Man
item.description: "It's honestly one of the more beautiful full circle moments I could've asked for." said the 6'11" Center Cisse.
item.dc:creator: Chase Izidoro, Fox 5

item.title: State Farm pulls Super Bowl commercial, shifts focus to supporting L.A. wildfire victims
item.description: State Farm has decided to pull its Super Bowl commercial and instead focus on supporting Los Angeles residents impacted by the deadly wildfires. “Our focus is firmly on providing support to the people of Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Montréal Canadiens hockey player struck by car while in Salt Lake City
item.description: The team later confirmed Emil Heineman suffered an "upper-body" injury and is expected to be sidelined for three to four weeks.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon Ducks 2024 receiver arrested as fugitive
item.description: Ryan Tristan Chance Taylor -- who played under the name Ryan Pellum -- allegedly used a 9mm pistol to assault someone on December 25 in Long Beach, California.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, NewsNation

item.title: MLB Players Trust hosts clinic for little leaguers
item.description: The rookies and baseball pros were all smiles during a special clinic to give back to the community and those affected by last year's hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Brew, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New England Patriots hire former Super Bowl champ Mike Vrabel as next coach
item.description: Vrabel, a fan favorite during eight seasons as a player in New England where he was a member of its first three Super Bowl winners and an inductee into the team’s Hall of Fame in 2023, was the preferred candidate because of his long association with the franchise and coaching success.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Red Rocks finish fourth in high-powered quad meet
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (ABC4 Sports) - The #4 Utah gymnastics team got a sense of where it stacks up early in the season against some of the top teams in the country.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Devin Booker scores 34 in Suns' 114-106 win over Jazz
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) - Devin Booker scored 34 points and the Phoenix Suns never trailed in a 114-106 victory over the Utah Jazz on Saturday. Booker had 22 points in the first half on 7-of-8 shooting, including 4 of 5 from 3-point range. He finished 12 for 20 overall and 6 for 12 from behind the arc.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
